result:
I’d change how people relate to truth—make it so we’re all a bit better at updating our beliefs when new evidence shows up, and a bit less attached to being “right.”

A huge amount of conflict, bad policy, and everyday friction comes from people doubling down instead of adjusting. If more of us were comfortable saying “I was wrong” or “I hadn’t thought of that,” you’d see ripple effects everywhere—science would move faster, politics would get less toxic, relationships would get healthier.

It’s not as flashy as ending disease or poverty outright, but it’s one of those leverage points. If people think more clearly and honestly, we’d make better decisions about almost everything else.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
